title: Bug fixes + opportunity score ranking for stock screener
observations: Screener's _analyze_one output dict was missing MC return fields (mc_p10_5y_return, mc_p90_5y_return, mc_median_5y_return), so compute_risk_reward in signal_analytics ALWAYS returned {available: False} in production — dead feature
Monte Carlo xi (vol-of-vol) parameter was hardcoded at 0.06 instead of reading from config — np.clip(0.06, min, max) ignores the config value
Risk scorer VIX floor used cascading pd.where calls that overwrite each other — VIX>30 tier was being replaced by VIX>25 tier
Signal conviction quality was using market_base as dominant driver when it should only look at stock-specific components
Stock router computed momentum from weekly-sampled price_history (sample_every=5) but used indices as if they were daily — 22 weekly samples = 110 trading days, not 22
Stock analyzer didn't recompute Ito correction when options calibration changed the vol — causing drift bias of 0.5*(old_vol^2 - new_vol^2)/year
Screener sorted by Sharpe only, ignoring signal direction — a Sell signal stock with high Sharpe ranked above a Buy signal stock
what_i_did: Fixed MC xi parameter: now reads garch_derived_params.xi from config instead of hardcoded 0.06
Fixed risk scorer VIX floor: replaced cascading pd.where with np.select for correct graduated thresholds
Fixed signal conviction dominant_driver: excludes market_base, uses only stock-specific components
Fixed stock_analyzer: recomputes Ito correction when options calibration changes vol
Added daily-resolution momentum fields to stock_analyzer output (momentum_1m, momentum_3m)
Updated stock router to use daily momentum from analyzer instead of computing from weekly-sampled data
Fixed screener to pass MC return fields to signal_analytics — risk_reward now actually works
Added prediction_confidence_score to screener output for opportunity scoring
Built compute_opportunity_score() — composite ranking metric combining signal score, Sharpe ratio, risk-reward ratio, and prediction confidence
Replaced Sharpe-only screener sort with opportunity_score sort
Added opportunity_score aggregate stats to signal analytics output
Wrote 14 new tests for opportunity score (differentiation, edge cases, monotonicity, clamping)

before.vix_floor: Cascading pd.where overwrites — VIX>30 shows as 0.5 instead of 0.8
before.conviction_dominant_driver: market_base could be dominant even for stock-specific signals
before.screener_sort: Sharpe only — Sell signal stocks could rank #1
before.momentum_accuracy: Off by ~5x (22 weekly samples = 110 days treated as 22 days)
before.options_ito_correction: Drift bias when options calibration changes vol

after.vix_floor: np.select: VIX>30→0.8, VIX>25→0.5, VIX>22→0.3 (correct graduated tiers)
after.conviction_dominant_driver: Excludes market_base, finds true stock-level driver
after.screener_risk_reward: Uses MC p10/p90 returns — available for all analyzed stocks
after.screener_sort: Opportunity score: signal(35%) + Sharpe(25%) + risk-reward(25%) + confidence(15%)
after.momentum_accuracy: Uses daily-resolution prices directly from stock_analyzer
after.options_ito_correction: Recomputed when vol changes — no drift bias

analysis.impact: High — 6 bug fixes (4 calculation accuracy, 1 dead feature, 1 momentum accuracy) plus a new composite ranking metric that makes the screener meaningfully smarter about stock selection
analysis.risk: Low — all changes are additive or fix correctness. No architectural changes. 197 targeted tests passing.
analysis.key_insight: The risk_reward feature was fully implemented but never worked because the screener didn't pass the MC return data through. This is a common integration gap: unit tests pass because they use mock data, but the real data flow is broken.
analysis.second_insight: The VIX floor bug (pd.where cascading) is subtle: pd.where(condition, value) replaces values where condition is FALSE, so cascading calls with progressively tighter conditions overwrite the previous tier's value. np.select evaluates conditions top-down with first-match semantics, which is the correct graduated behavior.

self_critique: The opportunity score weights (35/25/25/15) are reasonable defaults but not empirically calibrated — ideally these would be backtested against forward returns
The risk-reward log transform in opportunity_score is a design choice that could be tuned (currently log base 10)
Didn't add opportunity_score_weights to config.py — it falls back to hardcoded defaults via config.get with defaults, which is fine but could be more configurable
next_steps: Backtest opportunity score against forward 3M/6M/12M returns to validate ranking quality
Add opportunity_score_weights to config.py for full tunability
Consider adding a confidence-weighted signal that adjusts composite_score by prediction_confidence
The return_model (quantile predictor) is still not integrated into the signal engine — its p10/p50/p90 forecasts could improve signal quality
